description_en: A monument (natural monument), the Shiraneaoi (Glaucidium palmatum) flower of Mount Nikko-Shirane. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as a natural monument, designated on 2014-01-17. It is located in Higashiogawa.
A monument (natural monument), the Hanasaku stone. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as a natural monument, designated on 2014-09-17. It is located at 1289 Hanasaku, Nakazori district.
A tangible cultural property (stone object), a Hokyoin pagoda. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Gozairi.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a horse-headed Kannon (Bato Kannon) at Gozairi. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Gozairi.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a single-figure dosojin (traveler's guardian deity) stone. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Suganuma.

name_en: Datsueba (Old Woman of the Underworld River)
description_en: A tangible cultural property (stone object), a Datsueba figure. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Suributi.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a horse-headed Kannon (Bato Kannon) at Hataya. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Hataya.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a group of one hundred Koshin stones. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Kuryu.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), the Jizo statue of Senkan Pass. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Kuryu.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), the haiku monument (kuhi) of Omido. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Kamiogawa.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a paired-figure dosojin (traveler's guardian deity) stone. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Kamiogawa.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a multi-storied stone pagoda (Koshin five-storied pagoda). It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Ikanmachi.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a Koshin stone pillar bearing a Shomen Kongo figure. It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Ikanmachi.

A tangible cultural property (stone object), a hundred-pilgrimage commemorative stone pillar (hyakuban-to). It is village-designated by Katashina Village as an important cultural property in the stone-object category. It is located in Tokura.

name_romaji: Toyama ezumen nimai tsuki tokiri dogu isshiki
name_en: Two Quarry Maps of Toyama with a Set of Whetstone-Cutting Tools
description_en: A tangible cultural property (craft), two quarry maps of Toyama together with a set of whetstone-cutting tools. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as an important cultural property in the fine-arts and crafts category, designated on 1978-07-18. It is preserved at the folk museum and natural history museum in Nanmoku Village.
A monument (historic site), Kurotakisan Fudoji temple. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a historic site, designated on 1978-07-18. It is located at 1266 Oshiozawa-Ko.

A folk cultural property, the puppets of Hoshio. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as an important tangible folk cultural property, designated on 1978-07-18. It is preserved at the folk museum, from the Hoshio hamlet.

A monument (natural monument), the great cedar of Onita Shrine. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a natural monument, designated on 1978-07-18. It is located at 1668 Onita-Koyamazawa, at Onita Shrine.

name_en: Semi Pool with the Basho Mound
description_en: A monument (place of scenic beauty), the Semi pool together with the Basho mound. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a place of scenic beauty, designated on 1978-07-18. It is located in the Toyama river zone (Iwadai, Sasahara, Kainashi districts).

A monument (place of scenic beauty), the Fuchigataki waterfall. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a place of scenic beauty, designated on 1978-07-18. It is located in the tributary area of the Hoshio River.

name_romaji: Ama no Iwato kagura kira to men
name_en: Costumes and Masks of the Ama-no-Iwato Kagura
description_en: A folk cultural property, the costumes and masks of the Ama-no-Iwato kagura. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as an important tangible folk cultural property, designated on 1984-03-19. It is located at 999 Hizawa.

A folk cultural property, a stone paired-figure dosojin (traveler's guardian deity). It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as an important tangible folk cultural property, designated on 1984-03-19. It is located at 999 Ozawa.

name_romaji: Hizawa Jinja honden munafuda gomai tsuki
name_en: Main Hall of Hizawa Shrine with Five Ridge Plaques
description_en: A tangible cultural property (building), the main hall of Hizawa Shrine together with five ridge plaques (munafuda). It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as an important cultural property in the building category, designated on 1984-12-17. It is located at 651 Hizawa.

A monument (historic site), the Tennyo cave bearing a rock-face inscription. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a historic site, designated on 1989-11-30. It is located at 1738-1 Ozawa-Otsu.

name_en: Collection of Documents Related to Toyama
description_en: A tangible cultural property (craft), a collection of documents related to Toyama. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as an important cultural property in the fine-arts and crafts category, designated on 2004-02-24. It is located at 346 Toyama.
A monument (historic site), the gravestone of Ichikawa Gorobei. It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a historic site, designated on 2004-02-24. It is located at 74 Hazawa.

name_romaji: Saihoji ato Chonen-mei Hokyoin-to tsuki Onin-mei hoto
name_en: Chonen-Era Hokyoin Pagoda at the Saihoji Site with an Onin-Era Treasure Pagoda
description_en: A monument (historic site), a Chonen-era Hokyoin pagoda at the Saihoji temple site together with an Onin-era treasure pagoda (hoto). It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a historic site, designated on 2004-02-24. It is located at 156 Oshiozawa.
A monument (historic site), the Hoshio wind cave (fuketsu). It is village-designated by Nanmoku Village as a historic site, designated on 2015-07-28. It is located at 430-2 Hoshio.
A tangible cultural property, the Dodo Kannon (Kanzeon). It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1968-04-23. It is located in Fujioka.

A tangible cultural property, the Oei-era pagoda of Zoshinji temple. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1968-04-23. It is located in Fujioka.

name_en: Stone Stupa Slab of Ikoji Temple
description_en: A tangible cultural property, the stone stupa slab (itabi) of Ikoji temple. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1968-04-23. It is located in Ushida.

A tangible cultural property, the stone monument of the Basho mound. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1968-04-23. It is located in Kaminohino.

name_romaji: Yoda Nobushige shojo narabi chigyo ategai-jo
name_en: Letter and Fief-Grant Document of Yoda Nobushige
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a letter of Yoda Nobushige together with a fief-grant document (chigyo ategai-jo). It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1968-04-23. It is located in Morishinden.
A tangible cultural property, a painting by Kikukawa Eizan. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1970-04-15. It is located in Fujioka.

A tangible cultural property, a haniwa in the form of a quiver (yugi). It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1970-04-15. It is located in Shiroishi.

A tangible cultural property, a thousand-sutra memorial pagoda (senbu kuyo-to). It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1971-04-16. It is located in Midorino.

name_en: Mathematical Votive Tablet of Akiba Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property, a mathematical votive tablet (sangaku) of Akiba Shrine. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1971-04-16. It is located in Higashihirai.

A tangible cultural property, the stone monument of the Tenpo famine. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1971-04-16. It is located in Ushida.
A tangible cultural property, the Otoji Tenman Shrine. It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1972-04-10. It is located in Kobayashi.
A tangible cultural property, the Itchome guidepost (dohyo). It is municipally designated by Fujioka City as an important cultural property, designated on 1972-04-10. It is located in Fujioka.
